markets.question: Will Max Verstappen achieve the fastest lap at the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves the fastest lap at the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix, scheduled for Mar 15, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Mar 22, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other."

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially credited with the fastest lap in the Final Classification published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The fastest lap must be set during the race itself; times from practice sessions, qualifying, or any other sessions are not considered.

If no driver completes a lap during the race (e.g., due to a red flag ending the race prematurely), this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will there be a safety car during the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a safety car is deployed at any point during the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix, scheduled for Mar 15, 2026.

The market will resolve to "No" if the race is completed without any safety car deployment.

If the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Mar 22, 2026, this market will resolve 50-50.

Virtual Safety Car (VSC) deployments do not count as safety car deployments for the purpose of this market. Only physical safety car deployments where the safety car enters the track will result in a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will there be a red flag during the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a red flag is shown at any point during the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix, scheduled for Mar 15, 2026.

The market will resolve to "No" if the race is completed without any red flag periods.

If the 2026 F1 Chinese Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Mar 22, 2026, this market will resolve 50-50.

A red flag shown during practice sessions, qualifying sessions, or any other session besides the main race does not count for this market. Only red flags shown during the Grand Prix race itself will result in a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.
